message:
fix(channel): propagate flush errors and requeue undelivered messages in fault channel

send(): When the reorder buffer auto-flushes on capacity, errors from
the underlying sender are now propagated to the caller with the
original value intact, instead of being silently discarded via
`let _ = self.flush()`.  This lets callers detect disconnection
or backpressure at the point of the triggering send.

flush(): On partial delivery failure (disconnect, cancel, or full),
undelivered messages are now pushed back into the reorder buffer
along with all remaining pending items via the `pending` iterator.
This preserves the eventual-delivery guarantee — callers can retry
flush() after resolving the backpressure/disconnect condition and
all buffered messages will be re-attempted.

Also adds #[allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening)] on flush()
where the lock scope is intentionally tight, and a new test
flush_requeues_messages_when_receiver_disconnects that verifies
buffered_count remains stable after a failed flush.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
